content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 24, 2006


Elizabeth Wagner Hull
Assistant City Attorney
City of Chula Vista
276 Fourth Avenue
Chula Vista, CA 91910

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-079

Dear Ms. Hull:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We must decline your request for
advice since that would amount to advice on past conduct. (See § 83114; subd. (b); regulation
18329, subd. (b)(8)(A)&(C).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the
requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation
18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written
advice to you at this time. Your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated
decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate
to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act
so mandated by section 83111.

Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your
review.

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-06-079
Page No. 2


If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: Whether certain past conduct by the requestor violated the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request involves analysis of past conduct that has already occurred. Providing advice on past decisions would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Political Reform Act as required by law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Request was withdrawn due to involving analysis of past conduct. Document explicitly cites Government Code sections 83114 and regulation 18329 as authority for declining advice.
